{{Has quick guide}}
{{External|rs}}
{{Infobox Quest
|name = What Lies Below
|number = 122
|image = [[File:What Lies Below.png|300px]]
|release = [[27 March]] [[2007]]
|update = What Lies Below
|members = Yes
|series = None
|developer = Danny J
}}
'''What Lies Below''' is a quest that deals with the [[Dagon'hai]], an order of powerful Zamorakian mages. During the quest you team up with the [[Varrock Palace Secret Guard]] to help capture [[Surok Magis]].

==Details==
{{Quest details
|description = A travelling trader has been stranded south of Varrock after his cart was attacked by local outlaws. He needs the help of a hero to recover his lost documents, but that's not all...

The Varrock Palace Secret Guard (VPSG) are on the trail of treacherous dealings with the Monks of Zamorak. Will you be able to help the VPSG and uncover what lies below?
|start = Talk to [[Rat Burgiss]] south of [[Varrock]].
|startmap = 3266,3333
|difficulty = Intermediate
|length = Short
|requirements = *Completion of the following quests:
**[[Rune Mysteries]]
*{{SCP|Runecraft|35|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}} {{Questreqstart|yes}}
|items = *[[Empty bowl]]
*15 [[chaos rune]]s
*Access to the [[Chaos Altar]] ([[Chaos talisman]] or [[Chaos tiara|tiara]], [[Catalytic talisman]] or [[Catalytic tiara|tiara]], or travelling through the [[Abyss]] or Temple of the Eye)
|kills = *5 [[Outlaw]]s ''(level 32)''
*[[King Roald]] ''(level 47)''
|recommended = *{{SCP|Combat|30|link=yes}}
*{{SCP|Mining|42|link=yes}} {{Boostable|no}}
*[[Weapon]], [[armour]], and some food if low leveled
*[[Bronze pickaxe]] to avoid going into the Wilderness (a bronze pickaxe can be obtained during the quest)
*Fast travel to several locations around [[Varrock]];
**Varrock square ([[Varrock teleport]])
**Outlaw camp ([[ring of wealth]] to the Grand Exchange, [[skills necklace]] to the Cooks' Guild or Varrock teleport if medium [[Varrock Diary]] completed)
**South of Varrock ([[Chronicle]], [[Combat bracelet]] to the Champions' Guild, or [[Senntisten teleport]])
**The [[canoe]] system (requires axe and level 27 woodcutting for the Dugout canoe)
|leagueRegion = <poem>
Not completable, as it requires access to Misthalin.
</poem>
}}

==Walkthrough==
===Rat Burgiss===
{{Map|name=Rat Burgiss|3266,3333|r=3|mtype=square|align=right|width=250|height=250|caption=Rat Burgiss's location.}}
Speak to [[Rat Burgiss]] on the crossroads between [[Varrock]] and [[Lumbridge]]. He tells you that a bunch of [[outlaw]]s have ransacked his trading cart and that he needs you to get back the [[Rat's paper|five papers]] they stole from him. He will give you [[an empty folder]] to keep them in.

{{Map|3122,3473|align=left|width=200|height=200|rectX=16|rectY=11|mtype=rectangle|caption=The outlaws' campsite.}}
The outlaws are located between [[Edgeville]] and the [[Grand Exchange]]. You can use a [[Skills necklace]] or [[Ring of wealth]] to travel towards them. Once at the [[Outlaw Camp|camp]], kill five [[outlaw]]s (level 32) to retrieve five of [[Rat's paper]]s, and then use them on the folder to get a [[full folder]].

[[File:What Lies Below - fighting outlaws.png|thumb|250px|Fighting the outlaws in their camp.]]
Return to Rat with the folder; he will then ask you to deliver a [[Letter to Surok|letter]] to [[Surok Magis]] in the [[Varrock Palace Library]]. Though written in a [[VPSG code|hard to read font]], the message is in English.

Head to the library, on the north side of the {{FloorNumber|uk=0}} of the castle, and speak to Surok Magis. He will destroy the letter and tell you that he can turn plain [[clay]] into [[gold bar]]s! He will need two items, an ordinary [[bowl]] and an [[infused wand]]. He gives you [[Sin'keth's diary]] and a [[Wand (What Lies Below)|wand]] to be infused with chaos.

'''Note:''' Despite what he says, you do not need a chaos talisman or tiara to infuse the wand if using a different method to access the altar.

'''Note:''' If you want to get kudos from the Varrock museum you'll need to get the book [[Dagon'hai History]]. You'll find it in one of the two bookcases farthest east. After the quest you can give the book to the [[Historian Minas]] on the {{FloorNumber|uk=1}} of the Varrock museum.

===Chaos Altar===
{{Needed|[[Wand (What Lies Below)|Wand]], 15 [[chaos rune]]s, and a [[chaos talisman]]/[[chaos tiara|tiara]]|recommended=A method of [[Varrock teleport]] to quickly return from the altar.}}
Entering the chaos altar with either a [[chaos talisman]] or [[chaos tiara|tiara]], or access to the [[Abyss]]. There are four ways to reach the altar:

====Option 1====
{{Needed|[[Chaos talisman]] or [[Chaos tiara|tiara]]|skills={{SCP|Mining|42|link=yes}} to excavate the statue}}

:'''Note:''' Catalytic talismans cannot be used for this option.

Following this option avoids the Wilderness entirely. Read the [[Sin'keth's diary]], and then go east of [[Varrock]]. Go south-west of the [[Lumberyard]] and west of the [[Earth Altar]]. You should find a [[Statue (Tunnel of Chaos)|statue of Saradomin]]. Talk to [[Anna Jones]], and she will give you a [[bronze pickaxe]]. You must use a bronze pickaxe to excavate the statue ({{SCP|Mining|42}}) to reveal the [[Tunnel of Chaos]] entrance. Enter the tunnel, and you will find that the [[Dagon'hai]] organisation lives there. Head north until you reach a portal. The portal leads to the second level of the Chaos Maze. Climb down a ladder to reach the [[Chaos Altar]].

====Option 2====
{{Needed|[[Chaos talisman]] or [[Chaos tiara|tiara]]}}
Travel through the Wilderness ([[Wilderness#Wilderness levels|level 10]]). Head to the [[Chaos Altar]] entrance. Once inside, make your way through the maze to the Chaos Altar.
[[File:Infusing wand.gif|thumb|150px|Infusing the wand.]]

====Option 3====
Use the Abyss to travel to the [[Chaos Altar]], which requires completion of the [[Enter the Abyss]] [[miniquest]]. Teleport via the [[Mage of Zamorak]] north of [[Edgeville]] to reach the Abyss. Your prayer will be drained and you will be [[Skull (status)|skulled]]. Make your way past [[Abyssal walker]]s (level 81) into the inner circle, from where you can teleport to the altar.

====Option 4====
This option avoids the wilderness. Travel to the [[Chaos Altar]] by entering the [[Guardian of Chaos]] at the [[Temple of the Eye (location)|Temple of the Eye]], which requires completion of the [[Temple of the Eye]] quest. To do this, join a game of [[Guardians of the Rift]] and wait for the Guardian of Chaos to be active, or use a [[Portal talisman (chaos)|Portal Talisman (Chaos)]] to access the Chaos Altar when the Guardian is inactive. This will teleport you close to the altar. To leave, go back through the portal and pass through the barrier at the entrance to Guardians of the Rift.

To reach the Temple of the Eye, use the [[Minigame Teleport]] for Guardians of the Rift. Alternatively, use a [[necklace of passage]] to teleport to the Wizards' Tower and enter the portal in the basement.

====Wand====
When you have reached the Chaos Altar, you must ''use'' your [[Wand (What Lies Below)|wand]] on the altar. This will absorb the 15 [[chaos rune]]s to turn the wand into the [[Infused wand]]. Return to the library of [[Varrock]], and talk to [[Surok Magis]] with the infused wand and an empty bowl. He will then give you [[Surok's letter]] for [[Rat Burgiss]]. 

'''Note:''' If you take Surok's letter to show [[King Roald]] before Rat Burgiss, you will learn about his guards' skills.

===Ending===
Head back to [[Rat Burgiss]], and deliver the [[Surok's letter|letter]]. You will need to return to Varrock shortly, so restock on teleport supplies if necessary. Rat Burgiss will explain to you that he is actually the commander of [[Varrock Palace Secret Guard]] and that [[Surok Magis]] is planning on taking over the Kingdom of [[Misthalin]] by using a mind-control spell on [[King Roald]], and that you accidentally helped him with that. Rat Burgiss will direct you to [[Zaff]], owner of the [[Zaff's Superior Staffs!|staff shop]] in [[Varrock]], in order to prepare a spell to counteract Surok's.

Talk to Zaff, who will explain his plan to you. Zaff will give you a [[beacon ring]] and [[Zaff's instructions|instructions]] on how to arrest Surok Magis. You do not need to equip the ring.

Get your gear ready for a fight. Head to Surok Magis (who is now wearing [[Dagon'hai]] robes), and talk to him. He will cast his mind-control spell on [[King Roald]] (level 47), who you will have to fight. When the King has low health, your character will say that ''"Now would be a good time to summon Zaff!"''. Now you can ''operate'' the Beacon ring to summon Zaff.

After being submmoned, Zaff teleports King Roald away and casts a spell on Surok Magis, permanently sealing him in the library. Zaff then casts another spell, and the library goes dark. When the light returns, all evidence of the battle has been cleaned up, and Zaff is gone.

:'''Note''': This event unlocks the ability to purchase [[Battlestaff|battlestaves]] from [[Zaff's Superior Staffs!|Zaff's Superior Staffs]], in addition to the discounted battlestaves from the [[Varrock Diary]]. '''[[Defence]] [[combat pures|pures]] may choose to stop the quest here to avoid gaining defence experience'''.

Return to [[Rat Burgiss]] to finish the quest.

'''Congratulations, quest complete!'''

<gallery>
What Lies Below - Fighting King Roald.png|The player fights King Roald.
What Lies Below - King Roald restored.png|Zaff casts a spell upon his arrival, restoring the King's mind.
What Lies Below - Surok teleblocked.png|Surok attempts to escape, and is stopped by Zaff.
</gallery>

{{clear}}

==Rewards==
{{Quest rewards
|name = What Lies Below
|image = [[File:What Lies Below reward scroll.png|centre]]
|qp = 1
|rewards = 
*{{SCP|Runecraft|8,000}} [[Runecraft]] experience
*{{SCP|Defence|2,000}} [[Defence]] experience
*The [[Beacon ring]]
*Access to a shortcut to the [[Chaos Altar]]
*Completing this quest awards the player with 5 [[Kudos]] which can be claimed by talking to [[Historian Minas]] in the [[Varrock Museum]], while holding the [[Dagon'hai history]] book.
*5 [[Battlestaff|Battlestaves]] in stock of [[Zaff's Superior Staffs!]] store (after defeating [[King Roald]] - the quest doesn't need to be completed)

Unlocks:
* [[King Roald]] accessible in the [[Nightmare Zone]].
}}

==Required for completing==
Completion of What Lies Below is required for the following:
* [[Defender of Varrock]]

==Transcript==
{{hastranscript|Quest}}

==Music==
*[[Alternative Root]] - [[Tunnel of Chaos]]
*[[Surok's Theme]]

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 9 November 2022
|update = Diversity & Inclusion Changes and Quest Speedrunning Updates
|change = The 42 Mining requirement was changed back to a recommendation.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 29 June 2022
|update = Unranked Group Iron Changes and More!
|change = The 42 Mining recommendation was unintentionally changed back to a requirement.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 27 April 2022
|update = Beneath Cursed Sands
|change = The 42 Mining requirement has been changed to a recommendation.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 23 March 2022
|update = Guardians of the Rift Launch
|change = Level 42 Mining is now required to start the quest.
}}
{{Subject changes footer}}

==Trivia==
*The 'Aphro-Dizzy-Yak' [[Surok]] mentions refers to "<!--[[wp:aphrodisiac]]-->aphrodisiac", something which causes sexual arousal.
*The woman who gives you a [[bronze pickaxe]] to help find the shortcut is named [[Anna Jones]]. If you talk to her further, she reveals her full name to be Louisiana Jones, a play on the famous movie hero {{wp|Indiana Jones}}.
*The [[letter to Surok]] obtained from [[Rat Burgiss]], if deciphered from the [[VPSG code|coded font]], foreshadows Surok's plan before it is fully revealed.
*The player character says "Won't he be disinclined to acquiesce to the request?" which is a reference to a line said by Captain Barbossa in ''{{wp|Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl}}''.
*If you have completed the [[Garden of Tranquillity]] quest, while fighting [[King Roald]], he will shout phrases like "I'm allergic to roses!" and "That garden was awful!", which is a reference to the quest. If [[Shield of Arrav]] is completed, he will also say, "A member of the Phoenix Gang?", even if the player is in [[Black Arm Gang]]. If [[Darkness of Hallowvale]] is completed, he will say, "You are with the Vampyres!".
*An update made it possible to buy staffs at Zaff's in Varrock without completing What Lies Below.
*After returning to Rat Burgiss with the full folder, he'll ask you to speak to Surok; your character will say, "All work and no pay makes (username) a dull adventurer," which is a reference to {{WP|All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy|an old proverb}}.
*By using [[Surok's letter]] on [[King Roald]] before delivering it to Rat, the player character and the king will have a conversation revolving around the king's inability to accept he is in danger.
*Chaos altar can be accessed through [[Guardians of the Rift]] minigame, so going into the [[Wilderness]] isn't required.
* Surok tells the player character that they must have a chaos talisman or tiara in their inventory to infuse the wand, but this isn't actually required: "Surok Magis: You will need to have the 15 chaos runes in your inventory. Make sure you also have either a chaos talisman or chaos tiara to complete the infusion."

{{What Lies Below}}
{{Dagon'hai}}
